noteP1: We picked fifteen companies from our portfolio that map to the priorities of Choice Hotels's information and technology organization: frontier AI and the infrastructure to run it economically, the data foundations underneath, and the security and developer tooling that let both move to production.
noteP2: Each card opens to a brief and the angle we think fits Choice Hotels best, and you can filter by category. Our full portfolio of 150+ active companies is linked in the top right. Whenever it's convenient, send back the ones worth a closer look.
portfolioIntro: Every company here works at the infrastructure layer, across AI, data, security, and developer tooling, with an angle on where it fits Choice Hotels's agenda. Filter by category, then expand any card for the brief.

anthropic.pull: The AI program at Choice Hotels sits inside a cloud-first lodging franchisor supporting nearly 7,500 hotels, hundreds of thousands of rooms and a large owner-operator ecosystem. In that setting, a frontier model has to be useful across knowledge work and software delivery without creating an unmanageable decision surface. Anthropic gives the information and technology organization one platform for reasoning over guest profiles, reservations, rates, property content, loyalty activity, franchise data, RFPs and service interactions, orchestrating bounded agents and assisting development across choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications. Claude's focus on steerability and predictable behavior is especially relevant where guest privacy, payment security, franchise consistency, agent governance and uptime cannot be bolted on later. The result would be a safer path from isolated copilots to production AI that improves guest relevance and hotel economics across the franchise system.
cases: Reason across guest profiles, reservations, rates, property content, loyalty activity, franchise data, RFPs and service interactions without forcing teams into a separate knowledge system.
Use Claude Code across choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications and the integration services behind them.
Keep human review, model policy and audit evidence explicit in high-consequence workflows.

wispr.pull: Across Choice Hotels, many small observations arise during discover-to-book, rate-to-revenue and hotel-question-to-action workflows and are easy to lose between the field, floor, meeting or call and the system of record. Wispr Flow can convert those spoken inputs into clean text and commands directly inside choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications. Because it is an input layer rather than another application, adoption does not require teams to change where work is completed. The strategic fit is simple: capture more context from franchise owners, hotel staff, revenue teams, digital teams, customer-care agents and engineers, with less typing, so downstream service and AI systems begin with a more complete record.
cases: Turn front-desk notes, owner questions, guest calls, maintenance reports and revenue discussions into structured entries while the context is still fresh.
Work inside choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications rather than creating another application to manage.
Create a richer decision trail for handoffs, analytics and supervised agents.

browserbase.pull: A meaningful share of Choice Hotels' work still crosses property portals, channel partners, travel sites, owner tools and hotel web applications. Those browser-bound steps are hard to automate reliably because they carry session state, changing interfaces and sensitive credentials. Browserbase matters as secure, programmable browser infrastructure for agents: it can let the information and technology organization automate web workflows and test choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications at scale without building a brittle fleet of headless browsers. The fit is strongest for repeatable, supervised tasks inside discover-to-book, rate-to-revenue and hotel-question-to-action workflows, where centralized sessions, logs and controls are as important as the automation itself.
cases: Automate bounded tasks across property portals, channel partners, travel sites, owner tools and hotel web applications without waiting for every system to expose an API.
Run repeatable browser tests across choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications before customer- or employee-facing releases.
Centralize sessions, credentials and execution logs for governed agent activity.

coder.pull: The teams maintaining choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications need fast access to complex toolchains, but local workstations make environments inconsistent and put source code, credentials and regulated data closer to the edge. Coder matters because it moves developer workspaces into centrally governed cloud environments. For Choice Hotels, that can standardize onboarding, reproduce builds and apply security controls to the place where engineers work, not only to the pipeline after they commit. That matters because the current agenda—moving from a completed all-in AWS migration to enterprise-wide AI across guest discovery, franchise operations, distribution and agent-enabled hotel tools—requires more teams to gain approved access to modern AI, data and platform tooling.
cases: Standardize cloud workspaces for teams building choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications.
Keep source code, credentials and approved AI tooling inside controlled infrastructure.
Reduce onboarding and environment drift across distributed, acquired or specialized teams.

token.pull: As Choice Hotels adds agents to discover-to-book, rate-to-revenue and hotel-question-to-action workflows, every useful workflow introduces a non-human principal with credentials, permissions and an owner—or an ownership gap. Token Security matters because it treats those identities as a distinct governance domain rather than an extension of employee access. The platform can continuously find property integrations, booking services, AgentCore identities, workflow bots and hotel agents, govern their lifecycle and enforce access based on intended purpose. That would help Choice Hotels scale automation while keeping the blast radius around guest, reservation, rate, property, franchise, loyalty and distribution data narrow and the audit trail clear.
cases: Inventory property integrations, booking services, AgentCore identities, workflow bots and hotel agents and tie every credential to an accountable owner.
Constrain agent access to the specific guest, reservation, rate, property, franchise, loyalty and distribution data required for each workflow.
Preserve lifecycle and entitlement evidence for audits, regulators and internal risk review.

fal.pull: Choice Hotels has practical multimodal needs across property imagery, personalized marketing, training video, virtual tours and guest content. fal matters because it gives product and engineering teams a high-speed inference layer for image, video, audio and voice models through simple APIs, without forcing each use case to become an infrastructure project. The information and technology organization could use it to prototype and scale bounded generative experiences alongside choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications, from content production to simulation or visual assistance. The strongest fit is where latency and iteration speed matter but the risk profile around guest privacy, payment security, franchise consistency, agent governance and uptime still requires approved models, controlled inputs and a clear review path.
cases: Embed image, video, audio or voice generation into new agent, booking, property, revenue, franchise and guest capabilities.
Prototype against multiple media models through one developer-facing inference layer.
Keep review, consent and brand or safety controls in the surrounding application workflow.

montecarlo.pull: Choice Hotels' AI and operating decisions increasingly depend on guest, reservation, rate, property, franchise, loyalty and distribution data. A broken pipeline, silent schema change or weak model input can therefore surface as a service, safety or operational problem rather than a data-team ticket. Monte Carlo matters because it unifies observability across data pipelines, systems, code, models and the agents built on top. The information and technology organization would gain a common view of lineage, incidents and downstream impact across guest search, revenue management, hotel operations, RFP response and virtual-teammate agents. That trust layer is central to production AI that improves guest relevance and hotel economics across the franchise system, especially where early detection and clear ownership are essential to managing guest privacy, payment security, franchise consistency, agent governance and uptime.
cases: Monitor guest, reservation, rate, property, franchise, loyalty and distribution data from source through dashboards, models and agent actions.
Trace incidents to the affected choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications and accountable engineering teams.
Prevent silent data failures from undermining production AI that improves guest relevance and hotel economics across the franchise system.

tonic.pull: The teams building choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications need realistic data, but using production copies of guest, loyalty, reservation, payment, employee and franchise data creates unnecessary exposure and slows access approvals. Tonic matters because it can generate synthetic, de-identified datasets that preserve the shape and utility of enterprise data for development, testing and AI work. For Choice Hotels, that would let engineers reproduce edge cases, train or evaluate models and refresh lower environments without moving identifiable records into places they do not belong. The result is faster delivery with fewer sensitive records outside production—important in an environment shaped by guest privacy, payment security, franchise consistency, agent governance and uptime.
cases: Replace production copies of guest, loyalty, reservation, payment, employee and franchise data in development and test environments.
Generate rare and edge-case scenarios for choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications and AI evaluation.
Reduce privacy exposure while giving teams faster access to useful data.

netbox.pull: Choice Hotels is a cloud-first lodging franchisor supporting nearly 7,500 hotels, hundreds of thousands of rooms and a large owner-operator ecosystem. That scale creates a persistent infrastructure-data problem: asset records, topology and configuration intent are often split across tools and local teams. NetBox Labs can centralize that context for AWS cloud infrastructure, hotel networks, franchise connections and corporate systems and expose it programmatically to operations and automation. That is useful for everyday network changes, but also for the agentic future—the safest infrastructure agent is one grounded in an authoritative model of devices, sites, addresses and dependencies. For Choice Hotels, that supports faster change with less operational ambiguity.
cases: Create a maintained source of truth across AWS cloud infrastructure, hotel networks, franchise connections and corporate systems.
Drive provisioning and change validation for a fully AWS-based application estate now standardizing secure agent infrastructure from intended state.
Ground infrastructure automation and agents in accurate topology and ownership data.

valar.pull: The economics of Choice Hotels' AI program will change as guest search, revenue management, hotel operations, RFP response and virtual-teammate agents move from pilots to sustained volume. Valar is designed for that stage. It schedules and batches inference across available accelerators inside the enterprise VPC, improving throughput and cost per token while preserving the existing security boundary. For workloads involving guest, reservation, rate, property, franchise, loyalty and distribution data, that architecture keeps model traffic, logs and policy enforcement under Choice Hotels' control. The result is a production inference layer that advances production AI that improves guest relevance and hotel economics across the franchise system while preserving control over scale and data locality.
cases: Run guest search, revenue management, hotel operations, RFP response and virtual-teammate agents inside Choice Hotels' own VPC and security boundary.
Increase usable accelerator capacity through scheduling, batching and routing.
Keep prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies under enterprise control.

echo.pull: The applications supporting discover-to-book, rate-to-revenue and hotel-question-to-action workflows depend on a deep open-source and container supply chain. Echo matters because it replaces vulnerable building blocks with maintained, hardened dependencies that can be consumed as standard inputs. That gives developers working on choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications a safer starting point and reduces the patch churn that pulls security and platform teams away from higher-order risks. It also strengthens auditability: the organization can show which approved components were used and how they are maintained across the lifecycle.
cases: Standardize hardened dependencies across booking, property, revenue, franchise, distribution and agent applications.
Remove known vulnerabilities before they enter application and agent build pipelines.
Use FIPS-validated options where stronger assurance is required.

act.pull: Choice Hotels' cloud estate now includes a fully AWS-based application estate now standardizing secure agent infrastructure, with services and agents communicating across boundaries that are difficult to understand from identity or network controls alone. Act Security matters because it learns how applications are intended to communicate, compares that with actual behavior and guides teams in closing unnecessary paths. The agentless approach could help the information and technology organization create business-context perimeters around choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications without redesigning the network first. That is a direct fit for guest privacy, payment security, franchise consistency, agent governance and uptime, where one over-permissioned pathway can expand the blast radius of a compromise.
cases: Map intended and actual communication across a fully AWS-based application estate now standardizing secure agent infrastructure.
Create least-privilege perimeters around choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications and their data services.
Reduce lateral-movement risk without deploying another host agent.

clover.pull: For Choice Hotels, shipping faster only works if security can understand the roadmap at the same speed. Clover Security integrates into the design and planning stage, where franchise owners, hotel staff, revenue teams, digital teams, customer-care agents and engineers are defining new agent, booking, property, revenue, franchise and guest capabilities. It can surface risks from the context already captured in tickets and documents, then return specific requirements before architecture and code harden around a weak assumption. The fit is strongest for choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications, where early decisions touch guest privacy, payment security, franchise consistency, agent governance and uptime and where a preventable redesign near release would slow the broader technology program.
cases: Review new agent, booking, property, revenue, franchise and guest capabilities while requirements and architecture are still being shaped.
Map security decisions back to tickets, design documents and accountable owners.
Reduce late-stage rework across choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications without adding another approval queue.

drata.pull: The assurance burden at Choice Hotels spans privacy, PCI, franchise controls, accessibility, cross-border data and third-party assurance, with evidence distributed across cloud platforms, business systems and external partners. Drata provides one continuous layer for interpreting those signals, assigning ownership and maintaining audit readiness. It can reduce repetitive evidence work around a fully AWS-based application estate now standardizing secure agent infrastructure while giving risk and technology leaders a shared view of control health. The value is not simply a faster audit; it is earlier visibility into where policy, system behavior or third-party posture has diverged from the standard required for discover-to-book, rate-to-revenue and hotel-question-to-action workflows.
cases: Continuously map controls and evidence across choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications and a fully AWS-based application estate now standardizing secure agent infrastructure.
Reuse assurance work across privacy, PCI, franchise controls, accessibility, cross-border data and third-party assurance instead of rebuilding it for every review.
Give leaders a current view of control drift, ownership and third-party risk.

orca.pull: The cloud-security problem at Choice Hotels is not a shortage of findings; it is knowing which combinations of exposure, identity, vulnerability and data actually matter across a fully AWS-based application estate now standardizing secure agent infrastructure. Orca Security connects those signals in one agentless platform and guides remediation by business context. For the information and technology organization, that can reduce coverage gaps across choiceEDGE, property, revenue, franchise, booking, loyalty and distribution applications and emerging AI workloads while avoiding host-agent overhead. The value is a clearer, faster path from a cloud risk to the owner and action required to protect discover-to-book, rate-to-revenue and hotel-question-to-action workflows.
cases: Gain agentless visibility across Choice Hotels' cloud estate and emerging AI workloads.
Prioritize attack paths into sensitive Choice Hotels data and critical applications.
Unify posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance remediation in one view.
